characters.name: Abel Goellner
characters.description: Abel Goellneris a guest starring character on the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorAlan Peterson. Abel Goellner is aRed Cellaffiliated operative who struck out on his own and specializes in moving the money of terrorist groups around the world.

characters.name: Abel Goellner
characters.description: Abel Goellneris a guest starring character on the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorAlan Peterson. Abel Goellner is aRed Cellaffiliated operative who struck out on his own and specializes in moving the money of terrorist groups around the world.

characters.name: Alex Udinov
characters.description: Alexandra Udinovis the daughter ofNikolai UdinovandKatya Udinov. Her father owned a company namedZetrovand was assassinated byNikita. After her rescue by Nikita, Alex is sent to one of her father's friends to be protected. Instead, he sold her to a sex slave ring. Years later, Alex escaped Russia, making it to America. There she encounters Nikita, who tells her ofDivisionand trains her so that together they could take it out. Alex lived in Russia on her family's estate. Her father taught her how to survive. His teachings were strict. He once told her that he had indeed killed before but only to protect his family. On the night her father died Alex assumed her mother had as well. She hid underneath her bed while flames enveloped her home. This event causes traumatic flashbacks. As a sex slave Alex became addicted to drugs, which Nikita helped her break during the course of her training. OnceAmandatakes over Division, Alex works with her to get revenge on the man that ordered the hit on her family,Sergei Semak, her father's best friend, in exchange for bringing in Nikita. She agrees to do so in anger because she learned that Nikita was the one who pulled the trigger on her father. Alex and Nikita eventually reconcile. Alex then tries to take control of Zetrov after revealing that she is alive to the world.Ari Tasarovordered Sergei to be killed, in hopes of Amanda and himself gaining Zetrov. Afterwards, Alex decides to hand over Zetrov to a close loyal family friend. She later assisted Nikita in her raid on Division, which ended successfully with the deaths of Percy and Roan. Ryan Fletcher is placed by the US government to be Division's Director. Alex is known to have many walls that protect herself from being caught emotionally off guard. Amanda even has trouble getting through to the real Alex. Amanda claims that. The girl's like one of those Russian dolls; you open one version of her, only to find there's another hidden inside her." She does not talk about herself or her past much. Though when she does, she talks to Nikita and later in the season even to Amanda, or it's shown through flashbacks. She also suffers from panic attacks when certain things trigger suppressed memories of her past. Despite Alex and Nikita's closeness, she sometimes lies to Nikita to hide her true feelings. She also uses walls to suppress the fact that she is Alexandra Udinov, and claims to just be Alex. Nikita taught her to hide away who she really is so she's more believable to Division. Though she eventually accepts that she cannot hide who she is, as it's also no longer needed to hide her true self. Alex also has a hard time conveying her emotions, such as if she likes a boy. She tries not to get attached due to it having dire consequences, though she does sometimes reveal her emotional state. Sean once jokingly said "Wow, those walls you got there are tough. What do you Russians build with anyway? Field stone. Petrified potatoes?" He claims that sometimes when he's w

Amanda claims that. The girl's like one of those Russian dolls; you open one version of her, only to find there's another hidden inside her." She does not talk about herself or her past much. Though when she does, she talks to Nikita and later in the season even to Amanda, or it's shown through flashbacks. She also suffers from panic attacks when certain things trigger suppressed memories of her past. Despite Alex and Nikita's closeness, she sometimes lies to Nikita to hide her true feelings. She also uses walls to suppress the fact that she is Alexandra Udinov, and claims to just be Alex. Nikita taught her to hide away who she really is so she's more believable to Division. Though she eventually accepts that she cannot hide who she is, as it's also no longer needed to hide her true self. Alex also has a hard time conveying her emotions, such as if she likes a boy. She tries not to get attached due to it having dire consequences, though she does sometimes reveal her emotional state. Sean once jokingly said "Wow, those walls you got there are tough. What do you Russians build with anyway? Field stone. Petrified potatoes?" He claims that sometimes when he's with her he can see a crack in her wall and she gives him a look that shows her pure self. Alex has a mature exterior, though sometimes when she opens herself up, she appear

characters.name: Amanda
characters.description: –Amanda to Nikita in "Masks," referring to the psychological manipulation needed for Alex Amandais the resident psychologist and official manipulator (as well as torturer) ofDivision.Percyis rumored to have built his entire operation around Amanda's methods and relies on her to get inside people's heads, both during torture and to break them down to believe in Division unconditionally. Amanda is a chamelonic character.  When first meeting new recruits she tells them to think of her as a friend and confidant.  Amanda spends a great deal of time with her charges, building up their mental resilience and teaching them how to conduct themselves during various missions. She also seems to enjoy grooming their appearances.  She wants them to feel comraderie with her, which allows her to get inside their heads even more. Amanda is shown to be extremely manipulative and cunning. Using psychology she is able to manipulate people so subtly they are not even aware she is influencing them. Amanda can outwit nearly anyone as she is able to predict their steps and manipulate them. Amanda is also sadistic and takes great joy in hurting and controlling others. She enjoys to torture physically and mentally as well. Amanda displayed unhidden pleasure when she had Nikita cornered and enjoys confronting her victims with their mistakes and confuse them. When Amanda is angry, or feels betrayed, the facade is dropped and reveals someone who could be described as a borderline sociopath.  She will torture anyone, no matter how long she has known them, if she thinks they are withholding vital information from her.  She can be particularly brutal: During one interrogation she smashed a man's fingers with a hammer and then inserted needles into his nasal cavities to penetrate his brain. She later became more ambitious and joined several groups to achieve her goals. While feigning loyalty she kept working to achieve power only for herself and was willing to do whatever it takes to get this done. In spite of her viciousness, however, there are moments when Amanda lets a more vulnerable and caring side of her personality emerge, and she has been shown to be capable of empathy and kindness.

characters.name: Andrea Kosov
characters.description: Andrea Kosovis a guest starring character on the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". She is portrayed by Canadian actorCéline Bonnier. A transfer from a Section in the Asia sector, Andrea Kosov is a profiler who is suspected of sabotaging the missions of operatives who have spurned her.

characters.name: Andrea Kosov
characters.description: Andrea Kosovis a guest starring character on the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". She is portrayed by Canadian actorCéline Bonnier. characters.name: Andy Kula
characters.description: Andy Kulais a guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorStewart Bick. Andy is a civilianNikitameets at the bookstores she works at during her time as part of theOversightpilot program to release operatives fromSection One. He like to drop by the bookstore and flirt with Nikita. He gives her his business card as a no strings invitation to call if she's ever interested in going out. She does eventually call. At the end of their date, they walk back to Nikita's apartment and Andy places a tentative arm around her. Seeing a promotional poster for a ballet performance, he asks Nikita if she has interest in the ballet. Nikita is non committal. When they arrive at her front door, Andy gallantly offers to walk her in but Nikita politely declines. She gives him a light peck on the lips and starts to walk away but Andy asks if he can call her the following day. "I'll be upset if you didn't," Nikita replies and continues on her way inside. After Nikita agrees to assistGeorgewith his plan to killOperations, she quits her job at the bookstore. On her way out, Andy enters and presents her with tickets to the ballet. Nikita rushes away and apologetically informs him that she can't attend.

characters.name: Angie Georgiev
characters.description: Angie Georgievis a guest starring character from the 1997 USA Network series "La Femme Nikita". She is portrayed by Canadian actorNancy Beatty. Angie is a nurse that is taken hostage byMichaelwhen he's left behind during a mission to destroy a chemical plant that left him severely injured. He hid in her car when she arrived to leave on her lunch break, Michael ordered her to take him to her home. She reluctantly helps him with his injuries. During a moment when Michael was weakened by pain, Angie makes a phone call for help, but Michael's words cause her to change her mind. She becomes a willing accomplice in Michael's attempts to contact Section One. She cites her belief that Michael works for an entity with values similar to that of her now deceased father. During his life, his work for individual rights was suppressed by the totalitarian regime of their home country. With his departure imminent, Michael offers to assist Angie establish a new life elsewhere if she chooses. WhenEgran Petrosiantracks Michael to Angie's apartment, they escape and rendezvous withNikitaandMadeline, who were in the city on a retrieval mission. They all retreat to a location outside the city to await extraction by Section. Petrosian follows them to their location. Severely outnumbered, Michael and Nikita debate the potential suicide implications of trying to buy the approaching Section helicopter more time. Angie overhears them and quietly exits their hiding location with one of their firearms. She fires upon the approaching military forces but Angie is immediately gunned down. Michael rushes out to try and help but he is also fired upon. Section's helicopter arrives and eliminates all the interference they encounter. Everyone boards the helicopter. While in the air, Angie's dead body is visible as it lies in the snow.

characters.name: Ari Tasarov
characters.description: –Ari to Nikita in "The Life We've Chosen" Ari Tsarovis a recurring character on the CW'sNikita. He is the head of the criminal organizationGogoland aDivisionrival. He is portrayed by Canadian actorPeter Outerbridge.

characters.name: Ari Tasarov
characters.description: –Ari to Nikita in "The Life We've Chosen" Ari Tsarovis a recurring character on the CW'sNikita. He is the head of the criminal organizationGogoland aDivisionrival. He is portrayed by Canadian actorPeter Outerbridge.

characters.name: Benjamin Kruger
characters.description: Benjamin Krugeris a guest character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita".  He is portrayed by Canadian actorChristopher Bolton. Kruger is a high school drop-out who drifts from job to job and lives alone. He capturesSection One's attention because of a genetic mutation he possesses that occurs roughly in one in fifty million individuals. Because of it, he has a perfectly developed, photographic memory.

characters.name: Carla
characters.description: Carlais a recurring guest starring character on the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". She is portrayed by American actorAnais Granofsky. Carlawas a carpenter and a tenant in the same building in whichNikitalived. She lives across the hall from Nikita and after she has settled in, Carla brings over a coffee plant as a welcome gift. As her first contact with anyone outside ofSection One, Nikita is immediately suspicious and initially standoffish. Realizing her mistake, Nikita quickly invites Carla inside her apartment. Most of Carla's conversations with Nikita revolve around her romantic entanglements. In one instance, Carla does question Nikita about the secrecy involving the mysterious phone calls she receives.

characters.name: Chuck
characters.description: Chuckis a guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorKent Staines. Chuck is aSection Oneoperative. He and a small team of other operatives are assigned to retrieve a nuclear trigger stolen by arms dealersJohnandHelen Wicke. Before the team's departure, Chuck exchanges good natured banter withMichaelabout a recent hair cut.Nikitafondly comments about a recent mission they were on together that went well, causing Chuck to note with regret that she wasn't joining them.

characters.name: Damon Aral
characters.description: Damon Aralis a guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorElias Zarou. Damon Aral is plant manager of the Geneflex, a pharmaceutical plant is being used as s cover to produce Gandium 4, a grade one bioweapon.Section Onenoticed activity that had them selling to company that fronts forRed Cell.MichaelandNikitaaccess the plant by posing as quality control inspectors. Aral is their contact onsite. Section later executes a mission that leads to the destruction of the Geneflex plant. Damon Aral is among the armed resistance they encounter. Damon Aral:"You and Nikita, does your relationship go beyond work?"Michael:"She's available."Damon Aral:(playfully) "Oh, I wasn't talking about her."Michael:"I'm...I'm straight."Damon Aral:(nonplussed) "Sorry."Michael:"Don't be."

characters.name: David Fanning
characters.description: David Fanningis a character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorDouglas O'Keeffe. David Fanningis a professional hitman, whose sheltered wife,Lisa Fanning, is seduced byMichaelto gain access to his well guarded computer files.

characters.name: David Fanning
characters.description: David Fanningis a character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorDouglas O'Keeffe. David Fanningis a professional hitman, whose sheltered wife,Lisa Fanning, is seduced byMichaelto gain access to his well guarded computer files.

characters.name: Dori
characters.description: Doriis a guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". She is portrayed by Canadian actorJennifer Finnigan. Dori was collateral from a closedSection Onemission that targeted her brother. She has a formidable knowledge of operating systems and due to her exposure during the Burn mission, she was recruited because of that skill set.Madelineintroduced Dori toBirkofffor use in his department. Dori was solicitous enough in Madeline's company but her true feelings were revealed after Madeline departed. "Bitch," Dori muttered under her breath. Almost immediately, Dori made it known that she was not interested in remaining at Section One. During a conversation between Birkoff andOperations, she made a voice recording of Section's head. She used it to gain access to Level 20, where she believed an exit existed. When she arrived at Level 20, she found a rooftop with a view of the city around her. Initially elated, she discovered it was a hologram. Dori tried to exit the level but was then intercepted by Madeline. Dori returns to Comm much more docile and ready take on any task Birkoff assigns.

Initially elated, she discovered it was a hologram. Dori tried to exit the level but was then intercepted by Madeline. characters.name: Dorian Enquist
characters.description: Dorian Enquista guest starring character from the 1997USA NetworkseriesLa Femme Nikita. He is portrayed by Ukrainian born Canadian actorGregory Hlady. Enquist is a long time greenlisted independent contractor with connections to several terrorist groups. He was contracted bySection Oneto obtain valuable Semtex for them. He tries to extort Section One for double the original price and then sells the Semtex to a third party. As insurance, Enquist manages to kidnapMadeline. He also manages to temporarily hijackMichael's communication equipment when he was sent on a solo mission to eliminate him and retrieve the material. Aware that the mission has been compromised, a solitary Michael goes into Mandatory Refusal. His one objective is to gain possession of the material--the Semtex. In opposition,Operationsorders that Michael be stopped to avoid Enquist killing Madeline. Enquist tortures Madeline and momentarily kills her by accident. He admires her strength and invites her to accompany him when he leaves. Madeline declines. Despite extensive efforts to stop him, Michael makes it onto Enquist's property. He impersonates the driver of Enquist's real buyer and ultimately kills Enquist at point blank range.

He tries to extort Section One for double the original price and then sells the Semtex to a third party. As insurance, Enquist manages to kidnapMadeline. He also manages to temporarily hijackMichael's communication equipment when he was sent on a solo mission to eliminate him and retrieve the material. Aware that the mission has been compromised, a solitary Michael goes into Mandatory Refusal. His one objective is to gain possession of the material--the Semtex. In opposition,Operationsorders that Michael be stopped to avoid Enquist killing Madeline. Enquist tortures Madeline and momentarily kills her by accident. He admires her strength and invites her to accompany him when he leaves. Madeline declines. Despite extensive efforts to stop him, Michael makes it onto Enquist's property. He impersonates the driver of Enquist's real buyer and ultimately kills Enquist at point blank range.

characters.name: Egran Petrosian
characters.description: Egran Petrosianis a recurring guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by English born Canadian actorNigel Bennett. Originally believed to be a high ranking official of an Eastern Bloc country, Petrosian is revealed to be a long time Deep CoverSection Oneoperative.

characters.name: Egran Petrosian
characters.description: Egran Petrosianis a recurring guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by English born Canadian actorNigel Bennett. Originally believed to be a high ranking official of an Eastern Bloc country, Petrosian is revealed to be a long time Deep CoverSection Oneoperative.

characters.name: Eric Webber
characters.description: Eric Webberis a guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorJaimz Woolvett. Eric is a member ofSection One's surveillance team. He is assigned to watchNikita. He reveals himself to her and of his intentions to escape Section One. After hours of surveillance, he has determined that he knows Nikita well enough to know that she has the same opinion about Section as he does and would welcome the opportunity to escape. He's developed a computer virus that will erase them from Section's database. "We'll be ghosts," he tells her. Eric also works onsite technical support, allowing him to be in the field during missions.

characters.name: Errol Sparks
characters.description: Errol Sparksis a recurring guest starring character from the 1997 USA Network series "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by English born Canadian actorJulian Richings. Errol Sparkswas the theatrical head of the terrorist groupGlass Curtain. For three years he kept capturedSection OneoperativeSimoneis prisoner and tortured her repeatedly.Simonewas the long thought dead wife ofMichael. In revenge for his abuses,Simonelocks herself away with Sparks to commit murder/suicide. He survives. She does not. He later resurfaces and infiltratesSection One's computer safety protocols to then determine its location. That discovery leads to the voluntary abandonment and destruction ofSection Oneto avoid further incursion by Sparks andGlass Curtain.

characters.name: Forrester
characters.description: Forresteris a guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorKedar Brown. Forrester is theSection Oneoperative who heads Munitions afterNikitasendsWalterto theFarm.

characters.name: Gail
characters.description: Gailis a recurring character from the 1997 USA Network series "La Femme Nikita". She is portrayed by Canadian actorTara Slone. Gailwas a part ofSeymour Birkoff's team in Comm. The two of them dated briefly before she broke up with him in favor of another operative. She later came to regret her decision, but Birkoff was reluctant to renew their relationship.

characters.name: George
characters.description: Georgeis a recurring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by actorDavid Hemblen. George was the head ofOversightand the former lover ofAdrian. He was alongside her when all the Sections were founded. George has his own doubts aboutOperationsandMadeline.

characters.name: George
characters.description: Georgeis a recurring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by actorDavid Hemblen. characters.name: Gideon Van Vactor
characters.description: Gideon Van Vactoris a guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by actorBill MacDonald. Gideon Van Vactorwas the leader of a terrorist organization who sought to broker an alliance between several anti-western groups, includingShining PathandHezbollah.Nikita's first mission involved her acquiring a PDA with crucial information about that alliance.

characters.name: Gideon Van Vactor
characters.description: Gideon Van Vactoris a guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by actorBill MacDonald. Gideon Van Vactorwas the leader of a terrorist organization who sought to broker an alliance between several anti-western groups, includingShining PathandHezbollah.Nikita's first mission involved her acquiring a PDA with crucial information about that alliance.

characters.name: Greg Hillinger
characters.description: Greg Hillingeris a recurring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorKris Lemche. Greg Hillinger was a hotshot hacker initially recruited temporarily as an operative forSection One. When his recruitment became permanent, he was later assigned toBirkoff's mission support section.  Hillinger's talents were an asset to Section but his ego, particularly his overconfidence in his own intelligence and his chronic neglect of his duties, ultimately led to his downfall and cancellation.

characters.name: Greg Hillinger
characters.description: Greg Hillingeris a recurring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorKris Lemche. Greg Hillinger was a hotshot hacker initially recruited temporarily as an operative forSection One. characters.name: Gregor
characters.description: Gregoris a guest starring character on the 1997 USA Network series "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorGeza Kovac. Gregor is the suspicious father ofJulia, a young girl who inadvertently ends up in the middle ofSection One. Seeing her,Nikitatries to warn her but she falls to the ground in the commotion. The team departs before Nikita can determine whether she's been killed. Back at Section, Nikita reviews mission tapes to help her determine Julia's condition, but she's unable to do so. Nikita returns to the site during the day and begins inquiring with the local businesses about her whereabouts. One of the individuals she approaches happens to be Gregor. He has Nikita kidnapped and interrogated. He and his associates fear that Nikita is a police officer and will bring unwanted attention to their family gypsies. Julia is present much to Nikita's relief but the girl claims no knowledge of the incident Nikita refers to. Nikita concludes that Julia was out without permission and she fears her father's anger. Julia's father prepares to release Nikita but an ill-timed phone callMichaelusing her codename ofJoséphinechanges his mind. He orders that Nikita be killed and her body dumped far away. Julia hides a pick for Nikita to free herself in the car she's in and she manages to return to Section One unharmed.

characters.name: Gregor
characters.description: Gregoris a guest starring character on the 1997 USA Network series "La Femme Nikita". He is portrayed by Canadian actorGeza Kovac. Gregor is the suspicious father ofJulia, a young girl who inadvertently ends up in the middle ofSection One. Seeing her,Nikitatries to warn her but she falls to the ground in the commotion. Julia is present much to Nikita's relief but the girl claims no knowledge of the incident Nikita refers to. Nikita concludes that Julia was out without permission and she fears her father's anger. characters.name: Helen Wicke
characters.description: Helen Wickeis a guest starring character from the 1997USA Networkseries "La Femme Nikita". She is portrayed by Canadian actorSherry Miller. Helen Wickewas the wife and business partner of arms dealerJohn Wicke. She became pregnant at a young age and gave birth while incarcerated. She was then forced to give up her newborn daughter. When she had the financial means, she spent countless resources searching for the child.Section Oneexploited Helen's need to reunite with her daughter by plantingNikitawithin reach of a private detective Helen had hired. Nikita was kidnapped and brought to Helen's home where she successfully convinced Helen she was her lost daughter. Helen was eager to make up for lost time and doted on Nikita. Once John Wicke was eliminated, Helen and Nikita were apprehended by Section operatives with the goal of acquiring information on a nuclear trigger that the Wickes were planning to sell. When Helen refused,Michaelordered for Nikita to be beaten in front of Helen. In anguish, Helen reluctantly gave up the trigger's location. While convalescing, Nikita asked Michael to let Helen believe that she was her mother, "for however long she's got."
